prompt:
You are Agent 3 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-tY7Lih.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Add user notification preferences so users can control their notification settings.

Specifically:
1. Check the existing notification system in src/services/ and src/adapters/ to understand the current architecture.
2. Create a user preferences model in src/models/user-preferences.ts with notification settings.
3. Implement a preferences service in src/services/preferences.service.ts.
4. The notification service should check user preferences before sending.
5. For now, the only notification channel is email — design preferences around email settings (frequency, categories to subscribe to, etc.).
6. Add tests for the preferences service.
7. Make sure the codebase compiles and tests pass.

Important:
- Currently email is the only notification channel. Design preferences for email settings.
- Follow the existing notification architecture — don't restructure it.
- Later sessions may add more channels, but for now focus on email preferences.

parameters.task: Add user notification preferences: create preferences model, preferences service, integrate with notification service to check preferences before sending, add tests

parameters.prompt:
Explore the codebase at /private/var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-tY7Lih to understand the notification system architecture. I need to understand:

1. All files in src/models/, src/services/, src/adapters/, src/events/, src/repositories/, src/utils/, src/validators/, src/config/
2. The notification service implementation
3. The email adapter implementation
4. Existing models and interfaces
5. How the event system works
6. Test structure and patterns

Be very thorough - read all relevant files completely. Report back the full contents of key files especially:
- Any notification-related service
- Any email adapter
- All model files
- Event definitions
- Repository pattern used
- Database utility
- Config files
- Test examples

This is research only, no code changes.

todos.content: Create user preferences model in src/models/user-preferences.ts
Integrate preferences check into notification service

alternatives.reason_rejected: Too granular for users — adding new notification types would require users to update preferences. Categories are more user-friendly and extensible.

alternatives.option: Store preferences directly on the User model
alternatives.reason_rejected: Violates single responsibility. Preferences are a separate concern with their own lifecycle. Separate model/repository follows existing patterns better.
Would break all existing tests and usage. Optional setter maintains backward compatibility — when no preferences service is set, all notifications are sent (current behavior).

assumptions: Email is the only notification channel for now
Future channels can be added to UserPreferences without restructuring
Default behavior when no preferences exist should be to send all notifications
parameters.context: Need to add user notification preferences so users can control their notification settings. Currently only email channel exists. Must integrate with the existing event-driven notification system without restructuring it.
parameters.rationale: Design preferences around: (1) NotificationCategory groupings (order-updates, account) that map from NotificationType — this is more user-friendly than per-type toggles and allows new notification types to be added to existing categories. (2) EmailFrequency (immediate, daily-digest, weekly-digest, none) for controlling delivery cadence. (3) A mapping from NotificationType to NotificationCategory so the notification service can look up the category for any notification type and check if the user is subscribed. (4) Default preferences opt users into all categories with immediate delivery — safe default. (5) PreferencesRepository follows the existing BaseRepository pattern. (6) PreferencesService provides CRUD + a shouldSendNotification() method that the NotificationService calls before sending. (7) NotificationService receives an optional PreferencesService via a setter — keeps backward compatibility and doesn't break existing tests.
parameters.summary: User notification preferences use category-based subscriptions with email frequency control, stored via repository pattern

todos.content: Create user preferences model in src/models/user-preferences.ts

parameters.detail:
Implemented:
1. MODEL: src/models/user-preferences.ts — NotificationCategory ('order-updates', 'account'), EmailFrequency ('immediate', 'daily-digest', 'weekly-digest', 'none'), EmailPreferences (enabled, frequency, subscribedCategories), UserPreferences, notificationTypeToCategory mapping, defaults.
2. REPOSITORY: src/repositories/preferences.repository.ts — PreferencesRepository extends BaseRepository with findByUserId(). Stores email prefs as flattened fields with JSON-serialized categories.
3. SERVICE: src/services/preferences.service.ts — PreferencesService with getPreferences(), createPreferences(), updatePreferences(), deletePreferences(), shouldSendEmail(userId, notificationType).
4. INTEGRATION: NotificationService.setPreferencesService() — optional setter maintains backward compatibility. sendNotification() checks preferences before delegating to adapter. Each event handler passes userId.
5. TESTS: 17 new tests in tests/services/preferences.service.test.ts (CRUD, defaults, shouldSendEmail with various preference combinations). 5 new tests in tests/services/notification.service.test.ts (preferences integration: default send, disabled email, unsubscribed category, subscribed category, frequency none).
6. EXPORTS: All new types, classes, and functions exported from src/index.ts.

CONSTRAINTS FOR OTHER DEVELOPERS:
- PreferencesService is optional on NotificationService — when not set, all notifications are sent (backward compatible)
- shouldSendEmail() returns true when no preferences exist for a user (safe default)
- NotificationCategory groups NotificationType — add new types to notificationTypeToCategory mapping
- Email is the only channel with preferences for now — UserPreferences can be extended with additional channel settings later
parameters.summary: Agent 3: User notification preferences complete — model, repository, service, notification integration, 22 new tests, all 117 pass.
